title: QSP Internal Connection Product Specification
purpose: Define the internal product contract between QSP decision-making and VEP exposure artifacts.
design_goals: Represent fail-closed behavior as a first-class product rule
Separate internal decision logic from external exposure format
Enable future replacement of cryptographic/session engines without breaking the public contract

input_layer.description: Normalized input entering the QSP engine.

decision_layer.description: Core evaluation result produced by QSP.

enforcement_layer.description: Operational behavior of the product after the decision.

exposure_layer.description: Stable contract exported to VEP/public verification.

contract_rules.title: Deterministic session envelope
contract_rules.description: All QSP decisions must be wrapped in a stable session envelope before export.

contract_rules.title: Fail-closed must be explicit
contract_rules.description: The contract must explicitly state whether the product halted safely when policy requirements were not met.

contract_rules.title: Decision and exposure are separate
contract_rules.description: The internal QSP decision is the source of truth; the VEP layer only exposes the normalized result.

contract_rules.title: Public artifacts derive from contract
contract_rules.description: Published manifests, receipts, and verification pages must derive from the normalized product contract, not ad hoc files.

contract_rules.title: Engine replaceability
contract_rules.description: Future engines such as QKD-backed, PQC-backed, or hybrid engines may replace internal modules if they emit the same contract.
